briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 21, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Accelerating Climate Crisis and Immediate Impacts
sections.content: Warnings have been raised domestically that the pace of climate change has doubled over the past decade [1, 5]. These accelerated changes are already impacting various aspects of our lives, with the Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency issuing a Japanese encephalitis advisory due to the early emergence of vector mosquitoes caused by climate change [6]. Internationally, unprecedented March heatwaves in Colorado and Minnesota have demonstrated the reality of climate change [27, 28], and it has been confirmed that the Arctic and Antarctic are warming and transforming much faster than other regions of the Earth [33]. Furthermore, an analysis indicating that 5 million tons of carbon dioxide were emitted in just 14 days due to the ongoing Iran war in the Middle East, rapidly depleting the global carbon budget, adds to the severity of the climate crisis [38].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strategic Efforts for Climate Crisis Response in Korea
sections.content: In response to the accelerating climate crisis, various efforts are underway domestically. This October, the 'National Climate Science Institute,' a national think tank for climate crisis response, is scheduled to launch [2, 8], and the 'National Ulsan Carbon Neutrality Science Center' has also broken ground, aiming for completion by December next year [9]. Gumi City is investing 130.2 billion KRW in the carbon-neutral transition of its national industrial complex [10], while Ulsan's chemical industry is attempting a new leap forward through AI and carbon neutrality [14]. These are examples of specific carbon neutrality implementation projects actively being pursued by various local governments and industries [11, 13, 16, 17, 18, 19]. Furthermore, policy support is being strengthened, with the South Chungcheong Provincial Council promoting an amendment to an ordinance to expand support for climate change-resilient crops [7].

sections.title: 🌱 Exploring Innovative Technologies and Adaptation Strategies
sections.content: Innovative approaches are gaining attention for adapting to and overcoming climate change. In Geumsan County, the importance of spring management in ginseng cultivation in response to climate change was emphasized, showcasing adaptation efforts in the agricultural sector [3], and internationally, new apple varieties suitable for the 'era of climate change' have been developed [24]. Regarding energy infrastructure, it has been pointed out that the 100-year-old existing power grid is like 'arteriosclerosis' for the climate crisis, making carbon neutrality impossible [4, 21], and the necessity of building new energy highways, such as AI-based 'super digital grids,' has been emphasized [4, 21]. Discussions are also underway on whether virtual power plants could be a solution to the challenges of connecting renewable energy systems in Asia [22], indicating active exploration of technological solutions for building future energy systems.

sections.title: 🌍 International Response and Complex Climate Issues
sections.content: In the international community, complex issues are emerging alongside various levels of response to climate change. Pennsylvania has expressed policy opposition by joining a lawsuit against the Trump administration's rollback of greenhouse gas regulations [29], and the case of a ski lift on Germany's highest peak being dismantled due to glacier melt demonstrates the direct impact of climate change on actual landscapes and industries [30]. Meanwhile, those concerned about the disruption caused by climate change are seeking solutions in virtual classrooms [26], and the importance of social awareness and education is growing, as seen in discussions about how the younger generation's awareness of the climate crisis will reshape future society [31]. However, the complex reality of energy transition is also being revealed, including social conflicts during the renewable energy transition process, such as local residents' opposition to the construction of a large-scale solar power plant in the UK [39], and controversies over attending climate conferences during a fuel crisis [37].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the accelerating pace of climate change and its diverse impacts, alongside strategic response efforts both domestically and internationally, technological innovations, and complex societal challenges. Comprehensive efforts to address the climate crisis are more urgent than ever. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Exclusive: War in the Middle East is draining the global carbon budget faster than 84 countries combinedThe US-Israel war on Iran is a disaster for the climate, according to an analysis that finds it is draining the global carbon budget faster than 84 countries combined.As warplanes, drones and missiles kill thousands of people, level infrastructure and turn the Middle East into a gigantic environmental sacrifice zone, the first analysis of the climate cost has found the conflict led to 5m tonne

summary.phenomenon: The ongoing US-Israel war on Iran in the Middle East has resulted in 5 million tons of CO2 emissions in 14 days, depleting the global carbon budget faster than 84 countries combined.
summary.cause: Military conflict activities, including the use of warplanes, drones, missiles, and infrastructure destruction, are the cause of large-scale CO2 emissions.
summary.outlook: This conflict has a catastrophic impact on the climate, turning the Middle East into a gigantic environmental sacrifice zone.
